text.title: Fan-out/fan-in and errgroup+semaphore bounded-parallel patterns
text.summary: Fan-out/fan-in for batch processing and the preferred errgroup+semaphore pattern for "all-or-nothing" bounded parallelism with first-error cancellation.

section.title: When to prefer errgroup over custom pool

rule.statement: Use golang.org/x/sync/errgroup for "first error cancels siblings" semantics. Use a custom worker pool only for long-lived consumers. Use a semaphore+stream for large collections where N is not known up front. Do not implement a custom pool when errgroup fits.
rule.rationale: errgroup handles WaitGroup, error propagation, and context cancellation in ~3 lines. Custom pools duplicate this logic with more opportunity for bugs (missing close(jobs), dropped errors).

rule.statement: Every test file that spawns goroutines must call goleak.VerifyNone(t) in TestMain or t.Cleanup. Run go test -race to detect data races alongside leak detection.
rule.rationale: Goroutine leaks are silent: tests pass, CI passes, but the service slowly exhausts memory in production. goleak catches leaks at test time where they are cheap to fix.
